name: Feuerzangenbowle
recipeIngredient: 1 Bottle of golden rum (at least 108 proof)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the orange peel, lemon peel, and cloves in a tea filter bag and tie it shut with white yarn.
2. Hang the tea filter bag into a copper kettle and pour in the wine.
3. Heat the mixture close to boiling point, but ensure it never actually boils.
4. Set the kettle in the center of your table so guests can watch the ceremony.
5. Use a "Feuerzange" or a barbecue grid (especially those used for herring) to hold the sugar hat safely over the kettle without touching the water.
6. Once ready, dim the lights.
7. Pour rum onto the sugar hat using a ladle and light it (the rum must be at least 108 proof).
8. Keep the flames going by adding more rum until all the sugar has melted and mixed into the wine.
9. Remove the "Feuerzange" and the tea filter bag with spices.
10. Serve in heat-proof glasses.
Variation: Add sugar and/or a small amount of orange juice (preferably freshly pressed) to taste.
